subject: make UEB setting for VID in line with VID project
commit_message:
make UEB setting for VID in line with VID project

change UEB config in Apps_Users_OnBoarding_Script.sql to be in line
with configration in VID project, this eliminate annoying
handleSesionUpdatesNative failed java.lang.NumberFormatException
error in VID application

Change-Id: I542211ecee32aadbae4af8bd1914887e5a668330
Signed-off-by: Krzysztof Kwiecien <krzysztof.kwiecien1@orange.com>

entries.message: Uploaded patch set 1.

Patch Set 1:

Build Started https://jenkins.onap.org/job/portal-docker-master-three-scm-verify-script/64/

Patch Set 1:

I've been working on similar issues this week.  
1) I fixed the ueb_url_list property but have not yet submitted the change.
2) I too would like to build etc.zip (instead of archiving it) but currently the LF deployment scripts pull etc.zip from git.

I hope to wrap up these changes in the deliveries folder in the next few days.

Patch Set 3:

Hi Chris,
1. ueb_url_list was critical in my case as I had changed vm_base_name (for one of ONAP instance) in HEAT template so vm1-message-router wasn't there. Of course when vm_based_name is standard (vm1) it should be any problem, but FQDN from DNS looks like more universal solution
2. in my patch the etc.zip is build from /delivery/etc directory, so there as completly in line but I would like to keep etc dir open and track the changes in it content. Of course any changes to files in etc directory should finished by creating the new etc.zip. And of course if etc dir will be present in git repo, we can make in future patch for the deployment scripts - but first this file should be here in this project 
3. the most important change is related to ueb configuration in sql file for VID app, as it generate some errors in VID app. It's look like this error is not blocking point, but it is annoying, noisy and make find where another problem with VID harder. I suppose that similar changes should be done for other app, but I'm not try it yet :-).

Best regards,
KK
Patch Set 3: Code-Review+1

Patch Set 3: Code-Review-1

I very much want your change for VID in the on-boarding script.  Will you please offer that as a separate change so we can merge it immediately?

I have downvoted this change for several reasons:

1.  I have new versions of some property files to offer, as well as a new build script.

2.  I would prefer not to have two copies of each property file - one unpacked, one zipped.  

3.  We should not have passwords on the disk and exposed.  

After this release week crunch is over, I want to figure out how to have one unpacked version of the property files in Git, and adjust the LF build and deploy scripts to copy property from there.  The thorny problem is that these files expose passwords to machines that are on the open internet, anyone can read them and misuse the rackspace database.  We must find a better solution.

I apologize that I am asking you again to abandon (parts of) a commit.
Uploaded patch set 4.

Patch Set 4:

No problem, it is what review process is for. I will wait for project proposal about new building scripts.

BTW: there is currently problem with openjdk-8 and ca-certificate-java from ubuntu repo, so there is no chance to build any project which is based on that Java version.

Patch Set 4:

The current build script starts from 14.04.  I don't know how to get this build working so this change can be merged.

For what it's worth, I will soon push a proposed revision that starts with 16.04.  It works, at least locally it runs successfully.

Patch Set 4:

FYI: a send the bug report to ppa/open-jdk team in Canonical about the problem with their last (from yesterday) package  build. I will monitor the progres their and if its start to work we can rebuild. Nevertheless I vote for migration to 1604...
BTW: this problem will broke whole new ONAP instalation because openjdk-8 is instaled in startup scripts for all ubuntu1404 VM

Patch Set 4:

It's seems that openjdk-8 is corrected now, so if you will rebuild the project it should be done without errors.

Best regards,
KK
